growth_records.medium: DBAF (DB bicarbonate mineral medium + acetate + fumarate)

sec.units: NA (fumarate reduced to succinate; not quantified)

growth_records.cite: Tabares et al. Adaptive responses of Trichlorobacter lovleyi to nitrite detoxification reveal overlooked contributions of Geobacterales to nitrate ammonification. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf054. [PMC11972089]
growth_records.snippet: cells doubled twice faster (1.93 +/- 0.06 h) than in control cultures with fumarate (4.3 +/- 0.4 h). Fig 3A: 20 mM acetate and 20 mM fumarate as electron acceptor.
growth_records.notes: Fumarate control condition (electron acceptor). Doubling time td=4.3+/-0.4 h reported (Fig 3A; that panel used 20 mM acetate + 20 mM fumarate). mu = ln2/td = 0.693147/4.3 = 0.1612 /h (calculated). Routine DBAF stock uses 40 mM fumarate (listed); the 4.3 h td specifically corresponds to the 20 mM acetate/20 mM fumarate Fig 3A culture. Base DB mineral medium composition only cited (ref 39), not given in full, so medium_composition_stated=false; only CaCl2 (120 mg/L), cysteine.HCl (2 mM) and bicarbonate buffering explicitly stated. Fumarate is partially assimilated as carbon (~60% acetate oxidation efficiency mentioned). pH not stated (CO2/bicarbonate buffered). No biomass (gDW) reported so specific mmol/gDW/h rates not derivable.

growth_records.medium: DBAN (DB bicarbonate mineral medium + acetate + nitrate), C/NO3 molar ratio 4

up.units: mM (provided; electron acceptor, reduced to ammonium via DNRA)

sec.units: mM (nitrate ammonified to NH4+; not numerically reported for ratio 4)
sec.method: stated product, not quantified for this ratio

sec.units: NA (acetate fully oxidized to CO2 in TCA cycle; ~75% of acetate diverted to oxidation)

yields.note: growth efficiency highest at 5 mM nitrate; ~75% of acetate diverted to complete oxidation to CO2, remainder to biomass

growth_records.cite: Tabares et al. Adaptive responses of Trichlorobacter lovleyi to nitrite detoxification reveal overlooked contributions of Geobacterales to nitrate ammonification. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf054. [PMC11972089]
growth_records.snippet: cells doubled twice faster (1.93 +/- 0.06 h)... 20 mM acetate and 5 mM nitrate, which correspond to a C/NO3- ratio (molar ratio of 4, or 3.8 mg acetate per mg nitrate)
growth_records.notes: Optimal DNRA growth condition (electron acceptor nitrate). Doubling time td=1.93+/-0.06 h reported. mu = ln2/td = 0.693147/1.93 = 0.3591 /h (calculated). C/NO3 molar ratio 4. Nitrate (5 mM) reduced to ammonium; acetate (20 mM) electron donor, ~75% oxidized to CO2 (stated), remainder to biomass. Exact consumed/produced N-species concentrations not numerically given for ratio 4 (Fig 4 shows ratios 1-3), so uptake/secretion left as provided concentrations. Base DB composition only cited (ref 39) -> medium_composition_stated=false. pH not stated. No gDW -> specific rates not derivable.

growth_records.medium: DB + acetate + 5 mM nitrate, molar C/NO3 ratio 1 (acetate-limited)

up.units: mM (provided; limiting electron donor, ~75% oxidized to CO2)
mM consumed (74% of 5 mM nitrate converted)
up.method: measured, batch endpoint (early stationary supernatant, HPLC)

sec.method: measured, batch endpoint (colorimetric ammonia assay, background-subtracted)

sec.units: NA (~75% of acetate oxidized to CO2 in TCA cycle)

yields.nitrate_to_ammonium_conversion: 3.1 mM NH4 from 3.9 mM nitrate consumed

growth_records.cite: Tabares et al. Adaptive responses of Trichlorobacter lovleyi to nitrite detoxification reveal overlooked contributions of Geobacterales to nitrate ammonification. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf054. [PMC11972089]
growth_records.snippet: Acetate limitation ... converting 74% (3.9 +/- 0.3 mM) of the available nitrate to nitrite (0.6 +/- 0.2 mM) and ammonium (3.1 +/- 0.3 mM)
growth_records.notes: Acetate-limited DNRA (Fig 4A), 5 mM nitrate, molar C/NO3 ratio 1 (acetate ~5 mM). Measured net batch changes: nitrate consumed 3.9+/-0.3 mM (74% of 5 mM), ammonium produced 3.1+/-0.3 mM, nitrite accumulated 0.6+/-0.2 mM. ~75% of acetate diverted to complete oxidation to CO2 (stated). Growth rate/doubling time not numerically reported for this specific ratio, only that growth efficiency was high. Concentrations are net batch changes, not specific mmol/gDW/h (no biomass reported). Medium base composition only cited -> stated=false. pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: DB + acetate + 5 mM nitrate, molar C/NO3 ratios 2 and 3 (nitrate-limited)

up.units: mM (10 mM at ratio 2, 15 mM at ratio 3; provided in excess)
mM (~practically all of 5 mM nitrate consumed)
up.method: measured, batch endpoint (HPLC); stated qualitatively 'practically all'

sec.units: mM (~practically all nitrate recovered as ammonium; approximate)
sec.method: measured, batch endpoint; qualitative 'practically all the nitrate as ammonium'

yields.note: nearly complete nitrate-to-ammonium recovery when acetate not limiting

growth_records.cite: Tabares et al. Adaptive responses of Trichlorobacter lovleyi to nitrite detoxification reveal overlooked contributions of Geobacterales to nitrate ammonification. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf054. [PMC11972089]
growth_records.snippet: Higher concentrations of acetate to increase the molar C/NO3- ratio in the 5 mM nitrate cultures from 1 to 2 and 3 ... promoted the recovery of practically all the nitrate as ammonium. The nitrite intermediate was also detected, albeit at l
growth_records.notes: Nitrate-limited DNRA (Fig 4B ratio 2 ~10 mM acetate, Fig 4C ratio 3 ~15 mM acetate), both with 5 mM nitrate. Text states practically all 5 mM nitrate recovered as ammonium (so NH4 ~5 mM, approximate/inferred from 'practically all'), nitrite <0.5 mM (explicit). Ammonium value not numerically stated, only 'practically all' -> flagged approximate, confidence medium. No growth rate/doubling time given for these ratios. Net batch changes, not specific rates (no biomass). Medium base cited -> stated=false. pH not stated.

up.units: mM (~most nitrate reduced; nitrite ~9+/-3 mM average across treatments)

sec.units: mM accumulated (~9 +/- 3 mM average for all 10 mM treatments)
mM (higher than stoichiometric expected due to cell lysis; not clean)

yields.note: growth rates and yields significantly decreased; nitrite accumulated above cytotoxic threshold (>1 mM); cells inviable, not propagatable

growth_records.cite: Tabares et al. Adaptive responses of Trichlorobacter lovleyi to nitrite detoxification reveal overlooked contributions of Geobacterales to nitrate ammonification. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf054. [PMC11972089]
growth_records.snippet: nitrite accumulated and growth rates and yields significantly decreased in cultures with 10 mM nitrate ... reductive conversion of most of the nitrate to nitrite (~9 +/- 3 mM, average for all treatments) ... Cells in these cultures were als
growth_records.notes: Suboptimal/toxic condition: 10 mM nitrate (Fig 4, Fig 5). Most nitrate reduced to nitrite which accumulated to ~9+/-3 mM (above cytotoxic ~1 mM threshold); growth rate/yield decreased and cells inviable. Enzyme-assay cultures were 30 mM acetate/10 mM nitrate harvested ~40 h. Ammonium detected higher than stoichiometric due to cell lysis (confounded, not usable). No numeric growth rate given. Net batch changes only. Medium base cited -> stated=false. pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: DB + 3 mM acetate + 5 mM nitrate (severe acetate limitation, C/NO3 0.6), no H2

sec.units: mM (accumulated to toxic levels; not quantified numerically here)

yields.note: low cell yields; ~55 h generation time without H2

growth_records.cite: Tabares et al. Adaptive responses of Trichlorobacter lovleyi to nitrite detoxification reveal overlooked contributions of Geobacterales to nitrate ammonification. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf054. [PMC11972089]
growth_records.snippet: culture conditions with 5 mM nitrate (3 mM acetate; molar C/NO3- ratio of 0.6) that led to the accumulation of toxic levels of nitrite and, consequently, reduced growth efficiency (~55 h generation times, low cell yields)
growth_records.notes: Fig 6A no-H2 condition: 3 mM acetate + 5 mM nitrate (C/NO3 0.6). Generation (doubling) time ~55 h reported. mu = ln2/55 = 0.693147/55 = 0.0126 /h (calculated). Nitrite accumulates to toxic levels, low ammonium/cell yields. N-species not numerically tabulated in text. Net values only. Medium base cited -> stated=false. pH not stated.

growth_records.medium: DB + 3 mM acetate + 5 mM nitrate + H2 (hydrogenotrophic, C/NO3 0.6)

sec.units: mM (ammonium yields stimulated by H2; not numerically given)

yields.note: H2 stimulated growth (~26 h generation time) and ammonium yields vs ~55 h without H2

growth_records.cite: Tabares et al. Adaptive responses of Trichlorobacter lovleyi to nitrite detoxification reveal overlooked contributions of Geobacterales to nitrate ammonification. The ISME Journal (2025). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wraf054. [PMC11972089]
growth_records.snippet: hydrogenotrophic conditions in these cultures provided excess electrons for the reductive detoxification of nitrite, and stimulated both growth (generation times ~26 h) and ammonium yields
growth_records.notes: Fig 6A +H2 condition: 3 mM acetate + 5 mM nitrate (C/NO3 0.6) with H2:CO2 (80:20) headspace as supplemental electron donor. Generation (doubling) time ~26 h reported. mu = ln2/26 = 0.693147/26 = 0.0267 /h (calculated). H2 alleviated nitrite toxicity, stimulated growth and ammonium formation vs no-H2 control (~55 h). Rates not specific (no biomass). Medium base cited -> stated=false. pH not stated.

yield_fit.note: qS = (1/Yxs)·µ + m_s  — linear fit of carbon-source uptake vs growth rate
